---
title: "Free Body Diagrams"
---

Recall the following table taken from the Hibbeler Statics textbook. It summarizes the constraint resultant forces and moments due to various types of supports.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/fbd1.png){width=80%}

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/fbd2.png){width=80%}

::: {.callout-tip title="Think!"}
**Question:** What is the general rule which encompasses all the cases in the above table and more?

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
Whenever translation in a certain direction is constrained, a constraint force in that direction enforces the constraint. Whenever rotation along a certain axis is constrained, a constraint moment along the same axis results.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-important title="Note! Notation"}
Good notation is important: in print, vectors are usually denoted by boldface, e.g. ${\bf v}$ or ${\bf F}$. In writing, vectors are either denoted by an overhead arrow, e.g. $\vec{v}$, or by a line or squiggle underneath, e.g. $\underline{v}$. We opt for the latter notation since we reserve the top of a vector for other accents.
:::

For a massless inextensible string, the tension is the same at all points along the string. 

For a frictionless massless pulley, the tension in the string on either side of the pulley is the same. 

## Exercises

*The following problems are from Set 00 – Free Body Diagrams. Gravity acts vertically down; all problems are set up in the vertical plane.*

**1.** Draw the FBD of the horizontal beam.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p1_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p1_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**2.** Draw the FBD of the horizontal beam.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p2_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p2_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**3.** Draw the FBD of angled beam $AB$.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p3_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p3_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**4.** Draw the FBD of the bar.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p4_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p4_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**5.** Draw the FBD of beam $AB$.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p5_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p5_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**6.** Draw the FBDs of disks $A$ and $B$ each separately.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p6_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p6_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**7.** Draw the FBD of the bar.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p7_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p7_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**8.** Draw the FBD of bar $AB$.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p8_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p8_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**9.** Draw the FBD of the horizontal beam. What are the forces acting at $A$ and $B$?

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p9_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p9_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**10.** The cylinder of mass $m$ is in equilibrium. What forces act on it? Gravity acts vertically downwards (same direction as the drawn force $\mathbf{P}$).

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p10_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p10_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**11.** Draw the FBD of the beam.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p11_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p11_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**12.** Draw the FBDs of bars $AB$ and $BC$.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p12_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p12_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**13.** Draw the FBDs of bars $AC$ and $ED$.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p13_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p13_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::

**14.** Draw the FBD of each pulley.

![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p14_setup.png){width=50%}

::: {.callout-note title="Answer" collapse="true"}
![](../figures/free_body_diagrams/problems/p14_fbd.png){width=50%}
:::
